I hate saying this but the Running Man game, in the book, is not some thing you "apply" for.  You take a series of tests over a period of days and you are chosen to participate in whatever the  games authority thinks you are best suited for , or as in Ben's case if the Network thinks you are a agitator or dangerous political element..you  get assigned the running man game.  If you amke it all 30 days you get 1 billion dollars.

A couple of things to remember.  When SK wrote this cameras were not ubiquitous like the yare now.  Video cameras were large builky expensive things not many people had.  Security cameras were rare, almost non existent outside of expensive closed loop security cameras some commercial business would have had.  No street cams, no atm cams, nothing hooked up to the internet.    Richards is required to film himself for 10 minutes twice a day and send the tapes to the network via postal service.  One of the main ponits in the book  is that we are never sure, but we suspect the network is sending the Hunters Ben's location from those tapes he must send back.
